paragraphs.en: Off the release of “Minions & Monsters,” the director Pierre Coffin reflects on creating the language of Minionese, partly inspired by Indian food.
paragraphs.zh: 在《小黄人和怪物》（Minions & Monsters）上映之际，导演皮埃尔·科芬（Pierre Coffin）回顾了自己创建小黄人语的过程，而这门语言的灵感部分来源于印度食物。

paragraphs.en: “The challenge of making a ‘Minions’ movie is that it’s filled with Minions,” Pierre Coffin was saying, at a totally normal pitch, as he fiddled around in the open kitchen of a light-filled studio on the second floor of his Paris apartment. “It’s my chambre de bonne,” he said, cheerfully. “Like Gene Kelly!” Coffin is an animator, a writer, and an inadvertent voice actor: more than fifteen years ago, while working on “Despicable Me,” the film that débuted the Minions, he accidentally invented Minionese, the little yellow protagonists’ massively entertaining language. Now, nearly six billion dollars later, no one is eager to mess with a winning formula, so he continues burbling forth “bello”s and “big boss”s and “Hello, papaguena! Tu le bella comme le papaya”s. Coffin has co-directed four of the films, including the first three “Despicable Me”s, but “Minions & Monsters,” now in theatres, is his first solo effort. It took some persuading. “Someone in a meeting says, ‘Oh, let’s make that character blue,’ and it becomes blue, just because some guy said so in a meeting,” he said. “I didn’t want to make anything Minions-related unless there was a spark.”
paragraphs.zh: “制作一部‘小黄人’电影的挑战在于，它满屏都是小黄人，”在他位于巴黎公寓二层、采光明亮、开放式厨房里闲聊时，皮埃尔·科芬这样说。“这是我的‘chambre de bonne’，”他开玩笑道，“像吉恩·凯利（Gene Kelly）那样！” 科芬既是动画师，也是编剧，还是一位“意外”变成了配音演员：十五多年前，在制作《卑鄙的我》时——这部电影让小黄人走进银幕——他一不小心就发明了小黄人语，这种语言是那群黄色主角们的“疯狂娱乐型”语言。如今，项目票房几乎已达六十亿美元，没人愿意去改动那套行之有效的公式，所以他仍在不断冒出“bello”和“big boss”，以及“Hello, papaguena! Tu le bella comme le papaya”。科芬共同执导了四部电影，其中包括前三部《卑鄙的我》，而如今上映的《小黄人和怪物》是他第一次单独执导。这背后花了一番说服。“开会时有人说：‘哦，那角色就做成蓝色吧。’然后它就变成了蓝色，仅仅因为某个人在会议上这么说，”他解释说，“我不想拍任何与小黄人有关的东西，除非它本身有亮点。”

paragraphs.en: Coffin found the inspiration he needed—where else?—at the movies. The latest installment takes the Minions to Jazz Age Hollywood, where, unhelped by the fact that not everyone can comprehend them, they catastrophically endeavor to make a talkie. The film is stuffed with references: Muybridge, Chaplin, Keaton, Lloyd. It also includes a few non-industry nods: one of the Minions is named Dick, after Coffin’s co-writer Brian Lynch’s dad. “You know those illustrated books for kids, like ‘Where’s Waldo?’ ” Coffin said. “When we started ‘Minions,’ I really wanted it to be like that, for there to be lots of things happening everywhere.” The approach, he explained, adds depth and life to the Minions’ milieu, but it’s not exactly Chekhov’s gun for cartoons. “Sometimes we have people doing totally inappropriate stuff in the background,” he said. “People will see it or they won’t, but, if they do, they might think it’s funny.”
paragraphs.zh: 科芬找到了自己需要的灵感——就会在电影里找呗。最新作品把小黄人带到了爵士乐年代的好莱坞，在那里，他们在并非每个人都能听懂小黄人语言的情况下，拼命尝试制作“有声电影”，结局却很“灾难性”。影片充满了致敬：穆布里奇（Muybridge）、卓别林（Chaplin）、基顿（Keaton）、劳埃德（Lloyd）都被点到。它还包括一些不属于电影工业的致意：有一只小黄人的名字叫 Dick，来自科芬的联合编剧布莱恩·林奇（Brian Lynch）爸爸的名字。“你知道给孩子看的那种找画面里的细节书吗，比如《Where’s Waldo?》吗？”科芬说。“我们一开始做《小黄人》时，我真的很想要这种感觉，让每个地方都发生很多事情。” 他解释道，这种做法给小黄人的环境增添了层次和生命力，但它并不等于动画版的“契诃夫之枪”。“有时我们会让一些角色在背景里做一些完全不合场景的事，”他说，“观众要么看见了，要么没看见，但如果看见了，他们可能会觉得好笑。”

paragraphs.en: He was taking sips of what he called “my crappy instant coffee” from a mug someone had bought for him in the Basque country. A cat named Ziggy circulated quietly nearby. “I’m gonna have half a croissant,” Coffin said.
paragraphs.zh: 他一边喝着自己所谓的“我那杯糟糕的速溶咖啡”，一边从一个有人在巴斯克地区给他买的杯子里小口小口喝着。旁边有一只叫 Ziggy 的猫静静地走来走去。“我要吃半个可颂。”科芬说。

paragraphs.en: At first, Minionese “was all made out of ‘P’s and ‘T’s and exploding letters, but then it became something else.” He continued, “It’s not really a language. It’s a melody that I struggle to express. It starts off ‘Blah blah blah blah blah,’ or literally, like, ‘Blah blah blah,’ right?” (He was doing all the “blah”s with different intonations, and the emotions were coming through with surprising clarity.) “If I need to put the accent of anger, for instance, I need to find a three-syllable word that will illustrate the ‘Blah blah blah.’ ”
paragraphs.zh: 他先说：“开始的时候，小黄人语全是‘P’和‘T’以及会爆炸的字母，后来它又变成了别的东西。”他接着说：“它并不真的是一种语言。我更想把它当成一段旋律来表达，我总也说不清楚。它最初像‘Blah blah blah blah blah’，也就是直接说‘Blah blah blah’，你懂吧？”（他说这些‘blah’时每次都换了不同的语调，情绪却清楚得出奇。）“比如说，我要表达愤怒的语气时，就得找一个三个音节的词来承载那个‘Blah blah blah’。”

paragraphs.en: A visitor, watching “Minions & Monsters” a few days earlier, had been able to pick some croutons out of the word salad: Shinkansen, Mbappé, Control Z. She had read that Coffin sometimes plundered Indian-restaurant menus for vocabulary. Did he keep a running inventory?
paragraphs.zh: 几天前来看《Minions & Monsters》的一位观众已经能从这场“文字沙拉”里捞出一些片段：Shinkansen、Mbappé、Control Z。她听说过科芬有时会从印度餐馆的菜单里搜罗词汇。他会不会一直记着一份持续更新的词汇清单呢？

paragraphs.en: Coffin handed over his phone. “I have two lists because one really got too big,” he said. There they were: the Book of Thoth of Minionese, the ars grammatica of the Banana Language. Tapioca, Patagonia, subito, slip, cacahuète, pistache, courgetti, paella, lasagna, asticot, Tapadaka. Placebo, loukoumi, kouglof, toda raba, Sharapova, tempura, cacao, platypus, shrub, castagnette, potato salada, potage piquant pékinois.
paragraphs.zh: 科芬把手机递了过来。“我有两份清单，因为一份真的太长了，”他说。它们就在那儿：小黄人语的《托特之书》，也就是香蕉语的《语法大全》。Tapioca、Patagonia、subito、slip、cacahuète、pistache、courgetti、paella、lasagna、asticot、Tapadaka。Placebo、loukoumi、kouglof、toda raba、Sharapova、tempura、cacao、platypus、shrub、castagnette、potato salada、potage piquant pékinois。

paragraphs.en: Minionese is a mélange of many tongues, including French, Italian, Spanish, and German (all which Coffin speaks, at least a little), and Tagalog, Japanese, Chinese, and Russian (which he doesn’t). The son of the French diplomat Yves Coffin and the Indonesian novelist Nh. Dini, he learned English as a ten-year-old in Detroit, where his father had been posted. “The first week we arrived, my dad was, like, ‘There’s this movie everybody’s raving about. We’ve got to see it,’ ” Coffin recalled. “So we end up in this huge theatre, and it was ‘Star Wars.’ Obviously, I didn’t understand a word, but I went home and started making sketches of all the ships and stuff.”
paragraphs.zh: 小黄人语是多种语言的混合体，既有法语、意大利语、西班牙语和德语（科芬至少会一点点），也有他不会的他加禄语、日语、中文和俄语。科芬是法国外交官伊夫·科芬（Yves Coffin）和印度尼西亚小说家Nh. Dini的儿子，他在底特律学英语，当时父亲被派去那里工作，他十岁时。科芬回忆道：“我们一来，第一周我爸就说：‘有一部片子大家都在疯传，我们得去看。’结果我们最后去到一个巨大的影院，放的是《星球大战》。显然我一句都没听懂，但我回家后就开始画所有飞船和那些东西的草图。”

paragraphs.en: The visitor was struck by the image of a young Coffin in a dark theatre, straining to piece together a language that might as well have been gibberish. She posited that the experience might have had something to do with Minionese, but he said he didn’t really see it. Blah blah blah blah blah blah blah. ♦
paragraphs.zh: 游客被一幅年轻的Coffin身处黑暗影院、努力拼凑出一种几乎听起来像胡言乱语的语言的画面所吸引。她猜想这种体验可能与“Minionese”有关，但他表示自己并没真切地看到过这种东西。blah blah blah blah blah blah blah。♦
